entries.title_raw: Help us seat Dex’s Founding Expansion Board
entries.excerpt_raw: Dex opens nominations for a Founding Expansion Board to steward an always-free, CC-BY library of artist-made video and audio. Directors, Advisors, and Observers; ~6 hrs/mo; remote welcome. Details at dexdsl.com/board.
entries.cover_image_alt_raw: Help us seat Dex’s Founding Expansion Board
entries.body_raw:
We’re opening nominations for Dex’s Founding Expansion Board. If you care about high-quality, artist-made video and audio living in the open under CC-BY 4.0—free, forever—this is your moment to help shape the institution artists will lean on. Read the press release here.

NOMINATE OR APPLY →

TL;DR
• We’re recruiting Directors (voting), Named Advisors (1-year), and Board Observers (preview).
• Expectation is about 6 hours/month, quarterly meetings, remote welcome.
• Nominate or apply via dexdsl.org/board. If you have any questions, email board@dexdsl.org.

Why this matters
Dex pairs production-grade capture with permissive licensing so artists, filmmakers, educators, and developers can reuse and remix without paywalls (with attribution). Now that our catalog has passed ~30 hours and ~12,000 download events, we’re ready to build the next chapter with a board that keeps the catalog clean, the credits honest, and the door open.

What you’ll do
Help shortlist balanced, mission-true candidates; advise on priorities; and steward releases—bringing high quality cinematic work to the commons for usage in real projects.

Who we’re looking for

  • Legal / IP (Creative Commons & open culture)

  • Finance / FP&A (Treasurer track)

  • Development (major gifts · university gateways · corporate)

  • Grants (grantwriting and underwriting quick grants)

  • Community / Arts-Tech (LA · FL · DC · Baltimore; remote welcome)

  • Director of Video (cinematic performance capture)

Proces‌s at a glance

  1. Intake & quick screen

  2. Closed-door briefing

  3. Short preview/task (30–60 days)

  4. Committee recommendation

  5. Board vote & onboarding. Full details and eligibility are on the board page.

Key dates

  • Nominations (soft deadline): November 15, 2025

  • Briefings: December 2025 – January 2026

  • Rolling appointments: from February 2026

  • Seating: Q1 2026

Dex sessions are published for public reuse under CC-BY 4.0.

NOMINATE OR APPLY →

next_entry.excerpt_raw: On Oct. 9 - 11, Dex Digital Sample Library, ​​the open-access collection of archived, commissioned Creative Commons samples, presents dexFest 2024, a three-day recording arts festival featuring local LA artists and renowned multi-instrumental improvisers.

entries.excerpt_raw: On Oct. 9 - 11, Dex Digital Sample Library, ​​the open-access collection of archived, commissioned Creative Commons samples, presents dexFest 2024, a three-day recording arts festival featuring local LA artists and renowned multi-instrumental improvisers.
entries.body_raw:
Wednesday Oct. 9 - Friday Oct. 11, 2024 from 6PM - 12AM PDT at LA Artcore

LOS ANGELES, CA – On Oct. 9 - 11, Dex Digital Sample Library, ​​the open-access collection of archived, commissioned Creative Commons samples, presents dexFest 2024, a three-day recording arts festival featuring local LA artists and renowned multi-instrumental improvisers. The festival is part of a recording initiative set out by the online library to establish a collection of Creative Commons recordings for artists and creatives to use in projects without fear of copyright infringement. Continuing the series of live events set out by the organization, this festival is intended to be held once a year every year as an accelerator for artistic talent.

Ticketinng
LINEEUP

The line-up includes 18 local acts, with artists like HAYES, Tim Feeney, Kozue Matsumoto, Matt LeVeque, Ivan Cunningham, Battaglia Teglia Duo, Paul Yorke, Cameron Church alongside exhibitions by visual artists like Shoshi Watanabe and talks by featured guests. Each act is presented as a live recording session, rather than a concert, positioning audience members as participants who are privy to the process of musical production.

As each act is recorded live for curation into the library collection, festival goers are able to revisit the event, while patrons outside of LA are given a chance to experience the festival at home. All of the recordings from the festival will be released under CC BY 4.0 International licenses allowing anyone to use any note, groove, or sound from the festival for any creative projects with the sole condition that they acknowledge the owners of the work (more info at dexdsl.com/copyright).  “We are really excited to present this line-up, full of such tremendous talent” says Dex Digital Sample Library artistic director Seb Suarez, “and doubly so, we are happy to offer these recording sessions to the general public, both at the time of recording and through our open-access collection.” 

ticketing | dexFest 2024

NB when paying, please see gratuity dropdown for Zeffy, it is not needed to process your payment.

In their previous season, the non-profit arts organization added recordings of Sky Macklay and Sam Pluta to their collection, as well as several other experimental sample and foley recordings like Printer Sounds by Cameron Church.

Through live events like their fundraising festivals for Kozue Matsumoto’s Japan Ensemble (Summer Giving at LA Artcore) and through their work promulgating Creative Commons licensure as a robust framework in the US arts sector, they’re laying down the groundwork for self-determination within local arts communities as well as nationwide. This event continues partnerships with LA Artcore and local artists Kozue Matsumoto’s Japan Ensemble and Shoshi Watanabe.

SAMPLE PERFORMANCES

Treglia Battaglia Duo

Oct. 10, 7:00 PM

  • Battaglia Treglia Duo is dedicated to exploring extended-duration performances employing sustained tones and drones within the framework of extended microtonal just intonation. Violet Battaglia is a sound artist, composer, violinist and improviser who invites listeners to connect with the inherent beauty and complexity of the world around us. She is currently pursuing her bachelor’s degree at California Institute of the Arts for violin performance and composition. Trevor Treglia is an American composer and synthesist whose work concerns text, microtonality, and alternative notation. 

Chat with Selected Artists (Treglia Battaglia Duo, Paul Yorke, Shoshi Watanabe, Seb Suarez)

Oct. 10, 8:30 PM

  • The End of Copyright: the artists explore the meaning of copyright in the digital age, the history of copyright, and the need for the emergence of new structures outside of copyright environments, like Creative Commons and copyleft. Why are PROs scared of CC?

EVENT DETAILS

dexFest 2024

Dates: Wed. Oct. 9 - Fri. Oct. 11, 2024 

Times: 6:00 PM - 12:15 AM PDT

Location LA Artcore, 120 Judge John Aiso St, LA, 90012

Ticketing: 

$20/day, $45/festival (GA advance); 

$25/day, $50/festival (GA day of);

Student rush pricing available with valid CA student ID at $15/day, $30/festival;

Ticketing through Zeffy.

Sponsorship packages available upon request. 

For more information, interviews, or comps, please contact Cameron at info@dexdsl.com

Wednesday Oct. 9 - Friday Oct. 11, 2024 from 6PM - 12AM PDT at LA Artcore

LOS ANGELES, CA – On Oct. 9 - 11, Dex Digital Sample Library, ​​the open-access collection of archived, commissioned Creative Commons samples, presents dexFest 2024, a three-day recording arts festival featuring local LA artists and renowned multi-instrumental improvisers. The festival is part of a recording initiative set out by the online library to establish a collection of Creative Commons recordings for artists and creatives to use in projects without fear of copyright infringement. For more information, interviews, or comps, please contact Cameron at info@dexdsl.com

entries.title_raw: DEX PRESENTS: SUM‏‏‎MER GIVING 2024 – LA JAPAN ENSEMBLE GALA
entries.excerpt_raw: The Japan Ensemble is hosting a fundraising gala presented by Dex Digital Sample Library at LA ARTCORE on Wednesday, July 17 from 7 PM to 9 PM. Click for tickets.
entries.cover_image_alt_raw: DEX PRESENTS: SUM‏‏‎MER GIVING 2024 – LA JAPAN ENSEMBLE GALA
entries.body_raw:
(LOS ANGELES, CA, June 11, 2024) The Japan Ensemble is hosting a fundraising gala presented by Dex Digital Sample Library at LA ARTCORE on Wednesday, July 17 from 7 PM to 9 PM. The night includes performances by the Japanese Ensemble, a film screening, a silent auction, and a satsuma-imo (Japanese sweet potato) taco dinner, which is included in the ticket price. Performances by The Japanese Ensemble, directed by Kozue Matsumoto (Shogun on Hulu/Disney, Ghost of Tsushima, Genshin Concert Orchestra), will include chamber works, a taiko performance, and a full ensemble concert.

Tickets for the gala are available below, with a flat ticket price of $125. Guests are encouraged to donate beyond the ticket price to reach an initial funding goal of $10,000, of which a majority will go toward a matching fund for the ensemble to match any donations made during the gala. Special $20 student pricing is available for any student who displays a valid CA student ID upon entry. 

This fundraising effort comes at the sudden news of the student ensemble’s funding being cut this Spring by the California Institute of the Arts, the ensemble’s original endorser.  “This should be a period of giving to all BIPOC communities, not the opposite,” a student member states. “We deserve to exist, and we deserve to be successful with what we have built, not have it taken away. We’re not going anywhere soon.”

The ensemble, active since 2015, has amassed many passionate students who call the group family. “Learning how to play the koto encouraged me to rediscover what it means to be a part of the wider AAPI community,” another student shares. “It was deeply influential for me to work alongside Kozue Matsumoto in this endeavor as I believe her musical experience plays an integral part in the preservation and cultivation of Japanese culture in the West.”

 

We appreciate any donations! If you’d like a direct link to ticketing instead, click here.

TICKETS

ABOUT THE ENSEMBLE


The Japan Ensemble, led by Prof. Kozue Matsumoto, is a group of dedicated student musicians based in Santa Clarita, CA, committed to celebrating and sharing Japanese music and culture through education and performance. The ensemble is proud to be part of such a vibrant local area and excited by every opportunity to engage with our community through music. 


The ensemble began in 2015 at CalArts, where Prof. Matsumoto teaches a class introducing students from diverse backgrounds to Japanese culture, traditions, and instruments. All of the ensemble’s current members began their journey in this class and fell in love with Japanese music under Kozue’s knowledgeable and passionate guidance. 


The ensemble members have devoted years to honing their skills on their instruments. Through our performances, we’ve formed close ties with communities across Santa Clarita and Los Angeles, reaching audiences at libraries, gardens, schools, and the community.

LA Artcore is located at 120 judge john aiso st, Los Angeles, CA, 90012.

For media inquiries, please contact info@dexdsl.com

entries.title_raw: Help us save a college Japanese Ensemble!
entries.excerpt_raw: Help us save a college Japanese music ensemble with a charitable donation today.
entries.cover_image_alt_raw: Help us save a college Japanese Ensemble!
entries.body_raw:
Recently, the CalArts Herb Alpert Music School announced a change to its budget, cutting parts of the World Music department to make way for new offerings in the program. One of the affected programs was the Japanese ensemble, which came to us to see if we couldn’t find a way to fund the group for another year of performances, recordings, and Japanese-American Culture.

What is it?

The CalArts Japanese Ensemble, headed by Kozue Matsumoto (Shōgun, 2024, FX/Hulu; Ghost of Tsushima, 2020, Sucker Punch Studios/Sony), is a consort made up of koto, shamisen, shakuhachi, and taiko players; students learn techniques, rehearse traditional and contemporary compositions, and obtain knowledge of cultural ethnology by partaking in this ensemble. Throughout the school year, they practice and perform throughout the city, sharing Japanese musical culture with the greater Los Angeles area.

Without funding, students now have to cover expenses like concert catering, instruments and instrument repairs, and travel costs.

What students say about Japanese Ensemble

“…I caught [my friends] practicing on the first floor and they allowed me to join them. That day we made a small trio of two shamisen and a single koto but I felt the connection my teacher was talking about and from then on I was fully invested in supporting the group any way I could.

“As classes started up I remember helping out the other shamisen students learn the instrument, teaching them in a similar way that my past teacher did and really establishing myself as a member of the group… This bond became so strong that I would later call the Japanese ensemble my family , having friends who supported me and cared about my wellbeing and vice-versa.”

– Sebastian Melendez, BFA student

What are we doing about it?

as part of our efforts to provide some emergency operational funding for the ensemble, we are organizing two events:

  • Matsuri, a festival of Japanese music and culture. April 24, 2024, 5PM-10PM PST, Mark Taper Courtyard, CalArts, 24700 McBean Pkwy, Santa Clarita, CA, 91355

    • Food, fortunes, and musical performances from the ensemble in a picnic setting. Takoyaki, yakisoba, and maid café offered; omikuji fortunes presented as well.

  • Japanese Ensemble x Dex Gala, (more info soon) a fundraising gala event, with dinner and performances by the ensemble. July 15, 2024 5PM-7:30PM PST.

over the next three months, we plan to raise at least $10,000 for the ensemble, which will provide funding for instruments and repairs, event expenses, and special projects like recordings and commissions.

a portion of that funding will come from a one-time matching fund that we will pool to donate to the ensemble at the fundraising gala, matching audience contributions.

What can I do to help?

1. Attend our events. The easiest way to see what the Japanese ensemble has to offer is by seeing them play for you in person.

Come to the Japanese Matsuri festival on April 24, free to attend (see left)

Come to our fundraising gala at LA Artcore on July 17, 2024 from 7PM-9PM. More info available here.

2. Donate to the program to our matching fund that we will present to the ensemble at our fundraising gala.

Both can be done by clicking the button below:

GALA TICKETS/DONATIONS

We’re hoping to raise at least $10,000 for Kozue and the Japanese Ensemble so that they may keep performing around the greater LA area, recording works, and learning about and sharing Japanese culture with the greater public; that way, students can continue learning traditional, contemporary, and experimental music on koto, shamisen, shakuhachi, and taiko.

What do they sound like?

We can’t help them without you! Consider donating today to help provide money for concert expenses, performances, instruments and repairs, and general operational costs.

GALA TICKETS/DONATIONS

entries.body_raw:
dex season two comes out today, and we begin our new year of programming with two performance videos from Sky Macklay and Ben Giroux. we also started a Patreon. read more below.

it has been a great first year of operations for this library. we managed to:

  • release more than 10 hours of content for our first season, with international artists supplying hundreds of gigabytes of commissioned content for your use.

  • secure initial funding from Peabody LAUNCHPad/The Johns Hopkins University through the Peabody Launch Grant.

  • secure tax-exempt status, allowing us to participate in the Google for Nonprofits program.

  • reach more than 12,000 visitors online organically.

getting this program initially funded was a success, but we need to keep going if we want to make this project an ongoing endeavor. to that end, we’ve started a Patreon page where our patrons can help fund our productions, expand the library, and help keep the digital lights on, and where individual donors can make no-frills substantial contributions to our mission. if you like what you see, consider making a tax-deductible* contribution to us below.

check out our patreon

now in our second season of programming, we are continuing our commitment to being a library of people, not just sounds. these next coming audiovisual samples are not just authentic, stylized films, but they provide useful content to sample (and as always, licensed for free under CC-BY 4.0).

ben giroux for dex season 2

our season 2 programming starts right now with Sky Macklay on prepared oboe, and continues with hours of high-quality CC-BY content. this first entry is quite special, however. with a paper towel core jammed in the oboe’s horn, Macklay provides both brazenly loud and imperceptibly soft moments, the most memorable of which are an analog ring modulation glissando, a coda of underwater reed sounds, and many, many multiphonics.

we can’t wait for you to see what we have coming next. in fact, for the next five weeks, there will be at least one new AV entry for dex, delivered on mondays, which will comprise the first half of our second season programming.

  • Sky Macklay, prepared oboe, April 14, 2024

  • Ben Giroux, multiple percussion, April 21, 2024

  • Max Coleman, bassoon and electronics, April 28, 2024

  • Clara Kelly, soprano voice, May 5, 2024

  • Cameron Church, printer, May 12, 2024

beyond digital programming, we plan on expanding into physical programming in our hub locations of Los Angeles, Baltimore/DC, and Orlando (more on that soon!).

ok, that’s dex season 2 part i. over and out! –Seb

 

*donations are tax-deductible within the United States.

entries.excerpt_raw: after much hype, we finally launched our library to the public with drop featuring 10 recordings from artists across world.
entries.body_raw:
to catalog | to dex notes

 

10 artists.

over 9 hours of footage.

after much hype, we finally launched our library to the public with drop featuring 10 recordings from artists across the world:

baltimore, md

  • midori ataka

  • sam pluta

  • ethan bailey-gould

  • emmanuel losa

  • qiujiang levi lu

  • cameron church

  • tyler jordan

  • sebastian suarez-solis

dortmund, germany

  • paul* hermansen

aomori, japan

  • jared murphy

we officially launched this repository with our first season 1 drop on June 1st, 2023, with a portal available to download audio and video files for each recording. what started out as a recording project at the Peabody Conservatory is now an open-access collection of new-music sounds, licensed for use under Creative Commons.

midori ataka
qiujiang levi lu
jared murphy
emmanuel losa
ethan bailey-gould
paul* hermansen
sam pluta
sebastian suarez-solis
tyler jordan
cameron church

we – cameron, tyler, and sebastian – started this library under the following ideals. 



I. THE PEOPLE WE ARE WILL NOT BE FORGOTTEN

I. THE PEOPLE WE ARE WILL NOT BE FORGOTTEN

the heart of sounds, of music, and of performance will always be the people that bring them to life. traditional sample libraries attempt to derealize this fact and strip the performer away from their connection to the instrument. this trend extends beyond music into the technological grasp over our lives, our labor, and our love.

dex digital sample library combats this with our focus on documenting the person performing over capturing the sounds dispelled from their instrument. what we offer is a collection of humans and what they wish to communicate through sounds, performance, and emotions. not only does this offer a more human approach to the sample library, but the freedom that this allows also returns some of the most experimental and expressive samples in the field.

II. FREEDOM AND ACCESSIBILITY WILL COEXIST

II. FREEDOM AND ACCESSIBILITY WILL COEXIST

the main object of any copyleft environment is to balance freedom of ideas with management of accessibility. ideas can and should exist in a freely dispersed environment, like a local library might embody when within its most charitable disposition. in the face of a global capitalist hegemony, we remain optimistic in the viability and security of our open access content. the more accessible the content is, the more tools are available in the people’s hands for expression, creation, and recombination.

although the sounds we record are experimental and boundary-pushing, the utilization of them for the user is simple and accessible. in our library one will find multiple levels, or "buckets", of samples, which are our different approaches to sample splicing. from the full unedited recording, all the way down to single notes, attacks, and moments, one will find a sample that is applicable to their medium.

these sounds will be free and will remain free, for all to use. we aim to end the need for paid sample packs and the commercialization of sounds. by remaining a non-profit funded by subscribers (which can influence the direction of our recordings) and institutional grants (with no influence on our recordings), we can continue to flood the scene with high-quality, useable sounds, giving away as much as possible.  


III. OUR LIBRARY WILL BE FOR EVERYONE

III. OUR LIBRARY WILL BE FOR EVERYONE

even within our first round of recordings, we have worked with a diverse community of musicians and performers. it is important to us to bring together a community that represents a variety of identities, styles of performance, and approaches to art. although experimental samples are the core of our library, this does not supersede the desire for the people we record to be true to their own selves. from early music to cybernetic music, we offer a diverse collection of art, philosophy, and performance. with the use of our samples, these approaches can be combined and reassembled to find new connections and links between thoughts and ideas that may at first seem to be in combat with each other. at our core is an attempt to bring together people and their work to find new modes of being and becoming.

for instance, compare the dex recording of midori ataka, an organist who performed pre-existing works of early music to modern styles, to the dex recording of sam pluta, peabody faculty and technical director of the wet ink ensemble, who interfered with crt television frequencies to create beautifully harsh drones and abrasive visuals. how might one combine samples of these two recordings, and how might the simultaneous interfacing create new modes for artistic exploration?


IV. OUR PROJECT WILL BE FOREVER BLOOMING

IV. OUR PROJECT WILL BE FOREVER BLOOMING

on a season-to-season basis, we are constantly looking for new ways to approach the art of sampling. longer improvised sets are the basis of our practice, but this is not the only way we approach sampling. take for instance our call for minute-long field recordings. in the future, we'll continue to question our approach to sampling as well as calls for commissions. look out in the future for calls regarding the interpretation of our sample library, perhaps to commission multimedia installations with our audiovisual samples.


we look forward to releasing new recordings for our next season one drop. in the next drop, be prepared for a stacked roster of new recordings: you’ll see more prepared instruments, qiujiang levi lu on voice with everyday object manipulation, bassoon + pedals, finishing off with sky macklay performing on oboe and lyn goeringer performing with cinder blocks.

thanks for being a part of this project! we can’t we to see how you use these files.

all very best,

cameron, sebastian, and tyler

we look forward to releasing new recordings for our next season one drop. in the next drop, be prepared for a stacked roster of new recordings: you’ll see more prepared instruments, qiujiang levi lu on voice with everyday object manipulation, bassoon + pedals, finishing off with sky macklay performing on oboe and lyn goeringer performing with cinder blocks.

cameron, sebastian, and tyler

entries.excerpt_raw: A tinkerer at heart, Sebastian performs on just about anything it can get its hands on, like…
entries.body_raw:
to catalog | to dex notes

 

A tinkerer at heart, Sebastian performs on just about anything it can get its hands on, like prepared viola da gamba or prepared zarb. To them, the word instrument has gotten too formalized. 

“We as artists should only require from ourselves that we create. I am not merely what I am – nor what I believe in or play, or write, etc. I am what I do. That’s why, to me, performance is everything. In an atomized world, our particularities are performances.”

THEIR OUTPUT

For dex digital sample library, Sebastian has recorded about 3 hours of materials for 4 recordings. You can check them out here:

  • prepared viola da gamba

  • prepared harpsichord

  • prepared zarb + digital signal processor

  • prepared piano + digital signal processor

 

to catalog | to dex notes

entries.excerpt_raw: Cameron’s sound world can be described in a myriad ways, but one that seems to fit best in regards to his dex recordings is…
entries.body_raw:
to catalog | to dex notes

 

Cameron’s sound world can be described in a myriad ways, but one that seems to fit best in regards to his dex recordings is in terms of dialectics. His performances are dazzling, combining disparate ends together to make one complete form.

Think you’ve heard what a dulcimer sounds like? Think again. Seen everything there is to see with piano performance? Look again. Cameron’s output is both inspiring and striking (pun intended), with new, explorative methodologies toward instrument construction, preparation, and performance. While the performances are experimental – and there is a definite air of the avant-garde at play here – the sounds he creates have a wide variety of perspectives, benefits, and uses.

HIS OUTPUT

For dex digital sample library, Cameron has recorded about 2 hours of materials for 2 recordings. You can check them out here:

  • prepared hammered dulcimer + digital signal processor

  • prepared piano + digital signal processor

to catalog | to dex notes

An ever-captivating improviser, Ethan Bailey-Gould pushes the boundaries of creative expression through his skillful exploration of both the electric guitar and guitar pedals as instruments. Ethans music transcends the confines of traditional instrumentation, inviting listeners to immerse themselves in vivid sonic landscapes that captivate the senses.

HIS OUTPUT

For dex digital sample library, Ethan has recorded about 20 minutes of materials. Check it out here:

  • electric guitar + pedals

 

to catalog | to dex notes

entries.excerpt_raw: When we came to Midori with an inquiry regarding a possible dex recording, we had no idea she would record what is…
entries.body_raw:
When we came to Midori with an inquiry regarding a possible dex recording, we had no idea she would record what is one of our flagship recordings of our first season 1 drop. Of course, we never doubted her – anyone who has seen her on the organ is aware of her most graceful command of the instrument.

We recorded her collection of organ music, titled “2 Organs”, on Thursday May, 11, 2023, on the Peabody Conservatory Griswold Hall organ, as well as Peabody’s portative organ. Accompanied by plaster casts of an early Greek frieze and a 16th century tapestry, this collection is drenched in drama, a sort of visual tension which mirrors the harmonic tension in Midori’s organ performance.

HER OUTPUT

For dex digital sample library, Midori has recorded about 40 minutes of materials. Check them out here:

  • 2 organs
entries.body_html:
When we came to Midori with an inquiry regarding a possible dex recording, we had no idea she would record what is one of our flagship recordings of our first season 1 drop. Of course, we never doubted her – anyone who has seen her on the organ is aware of her most graceful command of the instrument.

We recorded her collection of organ music, titled “2 Organs”, on Thursday May, 11, 2023, on the Peabody Conservatory Griswold Hall organ, as well as Peabody’s portative organ. Accompanied by plaster casts of an early Greek frieze and a 16th century tapestry, this collection is drenched in drama, a sort of visual tension which mirrors the harmonic tension in Midori’s organ performance.

HER OUTPUT

For dex digital sample library, Midori has recorded about 40 minutes of materials. Check them out here:

  • 2 organs
